curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: I actually enjoy a budget hearing. I know that makes me a strange person. My wife has confirmed this on multiple occasions.
quotes.speaker: Paul Ryan
quotes.context: Interview with Wisconsin Public Radio during a tour of his Janesville district office, August 2015
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Ryan's wonky enthusiasm for budget math is completely documented and he often leavened it with self-aware domesticity. The slight tell is the spousal punchline, which is a little too sitcom-tidy for his usual delivery.

quotes.text: They are determined to get back in the fight
quotes.speaker: Sean Parnell
quotes.context: Pentagon spokesperson statement on injured service members after Iran strikes

source.title: Politics — Pentagon confirms nearly 100 service members injured since Iran strikes resumed

quotes.text: Worst damnfool mistake I ever made was letting myself be elected Vice President of the United States. Should have stuck with my old chores as Speaker of the House. I gave up the second most important job in the Government for one that didn't amount to a hill of beans. I spent eight long years as Mr. Roosevelt's spare tire.
quotes.speaker: John Nance Garner
quotes.context: FDR's first vice president ("Cactus Jack"), ruefully mocking his own decision to take the job and his powerlessness in it, after leaving office.

quotes.text: I spent years thinking the key to bipartisanship was finding the right policy. Turns out it's more about finding a Tuesday when nobody has a fundraiser.
quotes.speaker: Olympia Snowe
quotes.context: Keynote address at the Bipartisan Policy Center annual conference, Washington D.C., September 2013
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Snowe's entire post-Senate career has been devoted to diagnosing congressional dysfunction and she speaks at exactly these kinds of centrist forums. The giveaway is the punchline is a fraction too polished for unrehearsed remarks; it sounds like something she might have written rather than said.

quotes.text: People ask me what I miss about the Senate and I always say the same thing: the parking. The Senate parking was genuinely excellent.
quotes.speaker: Joe Biden
quotes.context: Remarks during a virtual fundraiser for the Delaware Democratic Party, March 2021
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Biden loves nostalgic Senate callbacks and deploys self-aware humor about trivial perks all the time. The giveaway is the punchline is *too* perfectly structured; his real riffs usually meander a bit more before landing.

quotes.text: Someone handed me a binder of talking points before a town hall once. I set it down, went out there, answered questions for ninety minutes, came back in, and the binder was exactly where I left it. I thought, well, that worked out.
quotes.speaker: John McCain
quotes.context: Conversation with reporters on the Straight Talk Express bus during the 2008 Republican primary campaign, as recounted in a March 2008 New York Times Magazine profile
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — McCain's disdain for pre-packaged messaging and love of unscripted town halls is legendary, and the Straight Talk Express setting is pitch-perfect. The tell is the story is a little *too* neat as a parable; his real anecdotes usually had a rougher edge.
